diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Abrupt on/off of a rapid regular tachycardia is characteristic of a re-entrant circuit.
Panic-driven sinus tachycardia rises and falls gradually rather than switching off abruptly.

symptoms.symptom_name: Perfectly regular (not irregular) fast rhythm sensation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Regularity favours a re-entrant SVT over ordinary atrial fibrillation.
Classic AF is irregularly irregular; a steady regular rate argues against it (though pre-excited AF remains the feared complication).

symptoms.symptom_name: Presyncope during the episode (had to lie flat)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Near-syncope during a tachyarrhythmia signals compromised cardiac output and elevates urgency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chest tightness and breathlessness during the high-rate episode

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ischaemic-type symptoms at very fast rates indicate the arrhythmia is not benign.
A recurring stereotyped paroxysmal tachycardia in a young person points to a fixed re-entrant substrate such as an accessory pathway.

symptoms.symptom_name: High stimulant intake (energy drinks / pre-workout / caffeine)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stimulants lower the threshold for and increase the frequency of paroxysmal tachyarrhythmias; a modifiable trigger, not a cause.
Distress about an arrhythmia is expected and must not anchor the assessment onto a primary psychiatric cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Structurally normal heart on examination — dual heart sounds, no murmur, no RV heave, normal symmetric pulses

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal cardiac exam with no murmur and preserved exercise tolerance makes structural disease less likely — but this is a sealed clinician measurement, not obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: Slurred upstroke of the QRS (delta wave) indicating ventricular pre-excitation
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Pre-excitation goes undetected and the accessory-pathway risk is never characterised.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A delta wave with a short PR interval is the defining pre-excitation pattern of WPW.

symptoms.symptom_name: Short PR interval on resting 12-lead ECG

investigation_details.expected_result: PR interval < 120 ms reflecting rapid accessory-pathway conduction

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A short PR interval reflects conduction bypassing the AV-nodal delay via the accessory pathway.

symptoms.symptom_name: Widened QRS complex on resting 12-lead ECG

investigation_details.expected_result: QRS widened by the delta wave (pre-excited ventricular activation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The widened QRS results from the fusion of accessory-pathway pre-excitation with normal conduction.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sudden switch-like onset/offset + regular rapid rate + presyncope + recurrence together strongly signal a re-entrant accessory-pathway tachycardia over panic or AF.
Short PR + delta wave + widened QRS is the pathognomonic pre-excitation pattern — obtainable only on the urgent resting 12-lead ECG, never via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single pivotal test — screens for the short PR interval and delta wave that confirm pre-excitation and change emergency management of future episodes.
Characterises the accessory pathway and enables potentially curative catheter ablation once pre-excitation is confirmed on ECG.
May capture paroxysmal episodes if the resting ECG is non-diagnostic; secondary to the urgent resting ECG.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine bloods for reassurance only (e.g. FBC) with no clinical indication
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Non-specific bloods do not diagnose an accessory pathway and must not delay or substitute for the urgent ECG.
